relapse_prevention_plan.title: Comprehensive Relapse Prevention Plan
relapse_prevention_plan.description: Warning signs recognition and evidence-based coping protocols for sustained recovery

stages_of_relapse.emotional_relapse: Poor self-care, not using coping skills
stages_of_relapse.mental_relapse: Internal struggle between wanting to use and wanting to stay clean

core_principles: Relapse is a process, not an event
Recovery is possible at any stage

early_warning_signs.description: Subtle changes indicating risk (Days to weeks before relapse)

signs.indicator: Canceling plans, not returning calls, avoiding support groups
Less than 6 hours or more than 10 hours consistently
Skipping meals, poor hygiene, disheveled appearance
Snapping at loved ones, road rage, emotional volatility
Skipping meetings, 'I got this' attitude, reducing support
Saying 'I'm fine' when clearly not, avoiding vulnerability
Quitting job, ending relationship, moving impulsively
Hungry, Angry, Lonely, Tired - basic needs unmet
Hiding phone, lying about whereabouts, sneaking

signs.intervention: Play the tape through, call sponsor immediately

signs.indicator: Just one time, special occasion, controlled use
I deserve this, I've been good, earned it
Going to old places, contacting using friends
signs.intervention: Leave immediately, call support, avoid triggers

signs.indicator: Physical urge, obsessive thoughts, tunnel vision
signs.action: Urge surfing, ice on face, intense exercise

signs.indicator: Driving to dealer area, buying substances 'just in case'
signs.action: Call emergency contact, go to safe place, 911 if needed

signs.indicator: Giving away recovery items, writing goodbye notes

steps.technique: Box breathing: 4 counts in, hold, out, hold
Leave location, turn off phone, go to safe space
Call sponsor, therapist, or crisis line
5-4-3-2-1 senses technique, cold water on face

strategies.name: Urge Surfing
strategies.description: Riding the wave of craving without acting
strategies.technique: Visualize urge as wave, observe peak and fall, don't fight it
strategies.effectiveness: Cravings peak at 20-30 minutes then subside

strategies.name: Opposite Action
strategies.technique: If urge to isolate â†’ call someone; if urge to use â†’ go to meeting

strategies.name: TIPP Skills
strategies.technique: Temperature (cold), Intense exercise, Paced breathing, Progressive muscle relaxation

strategies.name: Play the Tape Through
strategies.technique: Imagine using â†’ consequences â†’ regret â†’ starting over

protocols.name: Schedule Restructuring
actions: Plan structured activities for high-risk hours

protocols.name: Trigger Mapping
actions: List 10 specific triggers from past week
Create avoidance plan for external triggers
Develop coping cards for internal triggers
Remove paraphernalia and substances from environment

protocols.name: Sleep Restoration
protocols.accountability: Report sleep hours to support person

meal_support.accountability: Photo log or meal completion tracking

safety_planning.support: Call crisis line or trusted person

emergency.911: Immediate danger to self or others

questions: Have I used any coping skills today?
Do I need to reach out for support?
What am I grateful for today?

immediate: Ensure physical safety (OD protocol if substance use)
Do not use again ('just one more')
reframe: This is a lapse, not a failure
Strengthen plan based on new information

title: Relapse Prevention Plan
description: Log warning signs, risk level, coping actions, and support steps to prevent relapse.

questions.question: Immediate coping actions you will use (next 60 min)
questions.placeholder: Example: breathe, leave trigger area, call support...

questions.question: Any safety step needed right now?
Expected barriers
questions.placeholder: What might get in the way?

questions.question: Backup plan if urges spike again
